_comment: Codex static review labels for the first v1.2 manual review queue. Labels are triage evidence, not confirmed upstream bugs.

reason: ret from ext4_ext_insert_extent is checked at fast_commit.c:1784 and then jumps to out, where ext4_fc_replay_add_range returns 0 at line 1834; cleanup is present but the error value is swallowed.
notes: Replay semantics should be checked before treating this as a confirmed bug.

reason: ret from ext4_ext_replay_update_ex is checked at fast_commit.c:1798 and then flows to out, which returns 0 at line 1834 instead of propagating the error.
notes: Lock cleanup is not the issue here; the remaining signal is error swallowing.

reason: ret from ext4_ext_replay_update_ex is checked at fast_commit.c:1819 and then flows to out, which returns 0 at line 1834 instead of propagating the error.
notes: Replay semantics should be checked before treating this as a confirmed bug.

reason: The outer dentry length branch is not itself an error exit; the actual allocation failure path at fast_commit.c:448 frees node at line 449 and restores the caller-held i_fc_lock at line 451 before returning.
suggested_rule_update: Model __track_fn callbacks as entered with i_fc_lock held and treat the final mutex_lock as lock-state restoration, not a leaked acquisition.
notes: Also needs nested-if path sensitivity so line 446 is not treated as the failing condition.

reason: On the !node->fcd_name.name path, kmalloc failed so no name buffer was acquired; node is freed at fast_commit.c:449 and i_fc_lock is restored at line 451 before return.
suggested_rule_update: Distinguish failed acquisition from held resources and model caller-held lock restoration in __track_dentry_update.
notes: No missing kfree or mutex_unlock on this path.

reason: On the !node path at fast_commit.c:437, node was not allocated. The mutex_lock at line 439 restores the i_fc_lock that this callback is documented to be called with; it is not a resource that should be unlocked before returning.
suggested_rule_update: Add a lock contract for __track_dentry_update callbacks: return with ei->i_fc_lock held.
notes: Acquire-failure plus caller lock contract false positive.

reason: The down_write at fast_commit.c:1779 is released by up_write at line 1782 before the ret check at line 1784; no rwsem is held when the path jumps to out.
suggested_rule_update: Normalize extra parentheses in up_write((&EXT4_I(...))) and update lock state before later checks.
notes: The paired error_swallowed candidate remains separately interesting.

reason: The condition at fast_commit.c:1789 is in a different branch from the earlier down_write block; the lock acquired at line 1779 is released at line 1782 before leaving that branch.
suggested_rule_update: Add branch-sensitive resource state so resources acquired inside the ret==0 branch are not carried into sibling branches.
notes: No rwsem is held on this path.

reason: This path reuses the same infeasible lock state: down_write at fast_commit.c:1779 belongs to the ret==0 branch and is released at line 1782 before any later sibling branch.
suggested_rule_update: Add branch-sensitive resource state for fast_commit replay paths.
notes: No missing up_write on this path.

reason: The ret check at fast_commit.c:1798 is not reached while holding the line 1779 rwsem; that lock was released at line 1782 inside an earlier branch.
suggested_rule_update: Do not carry resources acquired in completed sibling branches into later branches.
notes: The error-swallowing aspect is covered by candidate_b068068e4066.

reason: The ret check at fast_commit.c:1819 is not reached while holding the line 1779 rwsem; the only down_write in this function is paired with up_write at line 1782.
suggested_rule_update: Add CFG/path-sensitive lock state for ext4_fc_replay_add_range.
notes: The error-swallowing aspect is covered by candidate_7acfe7ef5393.

reason: ext4_ind_trunc_restart_fn drops i_data_sem and sets dropped; ext4_ind_truncate_ensure_credits reacquires it at indirect.c:743 to restore the caller-held truncate lock before returning.
suggested_rule_update: Model ext4_ind_truncate_ensure_credits as preserving caller-held i_data_sem across journal restart.
notes: Returning with the lock held is the function contract, not a local leak.

reason: The bh branch after indirect.c:746 runs after the helper has restored the caller-held i_data_sem when dropped; errors return to a caller that owns the lock lifecycle.
suggested_rule_update: Add a lock-contract summary for ext4_ind_truncate_ensure_credits.
notes: No local up_write is expected in this helper.

reason: The unlikely(ret) path at indirect.c:750 returns while preserving the caller-held i_data_sem after a possible journal restart; this helper is not the final unlock owner.
suggested_rule_update: Represent caller-owned lock restoration in truncate helper summaries.
notes: No missing cleanup in the reviewed function contract.

reason: The read lock acquired at inode.c:548 is released at line 575 before the found label and before the block-validity check at line 578.
suggested_rule_update: Normalize up_read((&EXT4_I(...))) as releasing down_read(&EXT4_I(...)) and apply the release before label fallthrough.
notes: No rwsem is held when returning ret.

reason: The ret != 0 return at inode.c:580 is after up_read at line 575; the apparent missing cleanup is caused by expression normalization around up_read.
suggested_rule_update: Normalize parenthesized up_read expressions against acquired rwsem expressions.
notes: No missing up_read.

reason: The lookup-only return at inode.c:585 is after up_read at line 575, so the read-side i_data_sem has already been released.
suggested_rule_update: Track up_read at line 575 across the found label before later returns.
notes: No missing cleanup.

reason: The retval > 0 mapped-block return path at inode.c:595 is after up_read at line 575; no read lock remains held.
suggested_rule_update: Normalize the up_read expression and maintain lock state across label fallthrough.
notes: No missing up_read.

reason: The return at inode.c:601 is after up_read at line 575; the model carried a released read lock past the release site.
suggested_rule_update: Apply the line 575 up_read cleanup before later create/conversion checks.
notes: No missing cleanup.

reason: The write lock acquired at inode.c:616 is released at out_sem line 687 before any return after check_block_validity, so the missing up_write report is an expression/label tracking false positive.
suggested_rule_update: Normalize up_write((&EXT4_I(...))) and associate out_sem cleanup with all post-allocation exits.
notes: No missing up_write on the reviewed path.

reason: ext4_bread releases the buffer head with put_bh(bh) at inode.c:897 before returning ERR_PTR(ret); put_bh is a buffer_head release wrapper.
suggested_rule_update: Treat put_bh as a definitive buffer_head release alias in protocol scoring, not only a weak exception hint.
notes: Wrapper summary already hints this; ranking should demote it harder.

reason: After ext4_bread stores the current block in oi->of_binfo[i].ob_bh at orphan.c:600, a bad magic check at line 610 jumps to out_free; the out_free loop starts with i-- and releases only prior blocks, leaving the current ob_bh unreleased.
notes: Corrupt orphan block magic should exercise this path.

reason: After ext4_bread stores the current block in oi->of_binfo[i].ob_bh at orphan.c:600, a checksum failure at line 615 jumps to out_free; the loop decrements i before releasing, so the current failed block is skipped.
notes: Corrupt orphan block checksum should exercise this path.

reason: s->base is freed by kfree(s->base) at xattr.c:2231 on cleanup; the model tracked the field allocation as variable base and missed the field alias.
suggested_rule_update: Normalize s->base/base field aliases and match kfree(s->base) to allocations stored in s->base.
notes: The branch at line 2066 is not itself a leaking exit.

reason: The cleanup path frees s->base at xattr.c:2231 unless it aliases bs->bh->b_data; in that alias case the memory is not heap-owned by this function.
suggested_rule_update: Model s->base as a field alias and the bs->bh->b_data case as non-owned memory.
notes: No missing kfree(base).

reason: The ext4_journal_get_write_access error at xattr.c:2086 goes to cleanup_dquot and then cleanup; cleanup frees s->base at line 2231.
suggested_rule_update: Connect cleanup_dquot -> cleanup and normalize kfree(s->base) for base allocations.
notes: No missing kfree on this path.

reason: The ext4_handle_dirty_metadata error at xattr.c:2122 goes to cleanup_dquot and then cleanup; cleanup frees s->base at line 2231.
suggested_rule_update: Follow cleanup_dquot to cleanup and match field alias cleanup for s->base.
notes: No missing kfree on this path.

reason: When s->base aliases bs->bh->b_data, cleanup intentionally does not kfree it at xattr.c:2230; otherwise cleanup frees the allocated s->base at line 2231.
suggested_rule_update: Model the bs->bh->b_data alias as non-owned buffer memory and s->base allocations as freed by kfree(s->base).
notes: No owned heap allocation is leaked on this branch.

reason: On ext4_rename's whiteout error path, ext4_whiteout_for_rename stops any current journal handle before returning an ERR_PTR; the non-whiteout ext4_journal_start branch at namei.c:3881 is mutually exclusive.
suggested_rule_update: Add a callee summary for ext4_whiteout_for_rename error handling and improve branch-sensitive handle ownership.
notes: No ext4_journal_stop(handle) is missing in ext4_rename for this error path.

reason: When wait is false, ext4_bread_batch intentionally returns with the successfully acquired bhs[] entries owned by the caller for asynchronous completion; the function is not the cleanup owner.
suggested_rule_update: Add an ownership-transfer summary for ext4_bread_batch(..., wait=false, bhs).
notes: Caller is expected to release returned buffer_heads.

reason: The buffer read failure path at inode.c:931 jumps to out_brelse, whose loop releases every bhs[i] at lines 939-941 and clears the array entry.
suggested_rule_update: Match array-element cleanup brelse(bhs[i]) against resources represented as bhs.
notes: No missing brelse on the error path.

reason: flex_gd is NULL on the kmalloc failure path at resize.c:245, so no allocation was acquired and no kfree is required before returning NULL.
suggested_rule_update: Suppress missing-cleanup candidates when the reported resource is the failed allocation result being checked for NULL.
notes: Acquire-failure false positive.

reason: s_ctx is assigned to fc->fs_private at super.c:2528, and out_free calls ext4_fc_free(fc) at line 2555; ext4_fc_free frees fc->fs_private at line 2017.
suggested_rule_update: Add ext4_fc_free(fc) as a wrapper that releases fc->fs_private ext4_fs_context allocations.
notes: No missing kfree(s_ctx).

reason: The partial-cleanup report misses that ext4_fc_free(fc) at super.c:2555 frees fc->fs_private, including s_ctx, before kfree(fc).
suggested_rule_update: Model ext4_fc_free as releasing the fs_private context and quota-name members.
notes: Partial cleanup is complete after wrapper expansion.

reason: The parse failure path at super.c:2536 flows to out_free, which calls ext4_fc_free(fc) at line 2555; that wrapper frees fc->fs_private at line 2017.
suggested_rule_update: Add ext4_fc_free wrapper summary for parse_apply_sb_mount_options.
notes: No missing kfree(s_ctx).

reason: The partial-cleanup report misses ext4_fc_free(fc): out_free calls it at super.c:2555 and the wrapper frees ctx/fc->fs_private at line 2017.
suggested_rule_update: Use ext4_fc_free wrapper summary when scoring both missing_cleanup and partial_cleanup candidates.
notes: Partial cleanup is complete after wrapper expansion.

reason: ext4_feat is a kobject; on kobject_init_and_add failure, ext4_init_sysfs calls kobject_put(ext4_feat) at sysfs.c:591, and ext4_feat_ktype.release frees it at line 496.
suggested_rule_update: Model kobject_put with a release callback as cleanup for kzalloc-backed kobjects.
notes: No missing kfree(ext4_feat).

reason: The ext4_xattr_set_entry error at xattr.c:2042 goes to cleanup, where kfree(s->base) at line 2231 releases the allocation represented by base.
suggested_rule_update: Normalize field alias s->base/base for kfree matching.
notes: No missing kfree on this path.

reason: After the xattr inode handling branch, all exits flow through cleanup, which releases s->base at xattr.c:2231 unless it aliases existing buffer data.
suggested_rule_update: Track s->base as the allocated object and match kfree(s->base) to base.
notes: The condition at line 2045 is not a missing-cleanup exit.

reason: The ext4_xattr_inode_iget error at xattr.c:2056 goes to cleanup after setting ea_inode NULL; cleanup still frees s->base at line 2231.
suggested_rule_update: Match cleanup kfree(s->base) against the tracked base allocation.
notes: No missing kfree on this path.
